sections.paragraph:
παιδόθεν adv. (Sb 5294, 8; Ps.-Lucian, Philopatr. 19; Themist., Or. 25 p. 310d; evidence for use Ibycus [VI b.c.] 1, 10 Bergk [=Diehl2 6, 12 v.l.], in Athen. 13, 601b, is questionable [L-S-J-M Suppl. deletes the ref.]; Sb 5294, 8 [III a.d.]) from childhood ἐκ π. (Laud. Therap. E, ln. 8) Mk 9:21 v.l. S. παιδιόθεν.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Themist Themist , IV a.d.—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

ref. ref. = reference(s)

  1. ln. = line

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 750
